---
name: dogfooding
description: "Use when: reviewing or testing the app as an end user to provide a critical dogfooding report on experience, fun, and improvement opportunities."
---

# Dogfooding Skill

This skill guides reviewers through a focused dogfooding workflow for the Bingo Mixer app.

## What it does

- Runs a structured playtest of the app from first launch through gameplay and win behavior.
- Evaluates game clarity, social feel, engagement, and overall fun.
- Produces a concise critique with strengths, weaknesses, and concrete improvement suggestions.

## How to use

1. Confirm the app is installed and can run locally.
2. Open the app and go through the full flow as if you were a first-time player.
3. Note first impressions for:
   - onboarding and instructions
   - board content quality
   - interaction feedback and affordance
   - win moment and replay feel
4. Identify any confusing or boring moments.
5. Suggest small changes that would make the game feel more playful or memorable.

## Criteria

- Is it easy to understand what to do?
- Does the game feel more like a checklist or a social icebreaker?
- Does the UI support quick, repeated play?
- Is the bingo win moment satisfying enough to feel rewarding?
- Are the prompt questions interesting enough for a lively mixer?

## Sample prompts

- "Run a dogfooding review of the Bingo Mixer app and tell me what feels fun or flat."
- "Evaluate whether this app would work well at an in-person icebreaker event, and explain why."
- "Play the app like a new user and summarize the most important improvements."

## Output format

- Short summary of the player experience
- List of what worked well
- List of the biggest blockers to fun
- Practical recommendations for the next improvement cycle
